description_full:
You'll conduct in-depth research on global flight markets, industry trends, and passenger demographics to provide valuable market intelligence and trend analysis to stakeholders.
You’ll monitor competitor activities across routes, analyzing fare structures, schedule changes, and promotional campaigns to maintain TUI's competitive positioning.
Screen capacity developments from airlines across markets and provide to Analytics function within Core services.
We'll count on you to screen and forecast capacity needs by monitoring airline schedule changes, new route announcements, and market developments to provide insights to our Analytics teams.
Conducting competitor analysis will be essential as you evaluate offerings, pricing models, route networks, and market positioning to identify competitive advantages and areas for improvement.
You'll provide data-driven recommendations for route development and optimisation, suggesting pricing strategies and identifying opportunities to enhance customer experience.
You’ll develop and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports tracking key performance indicators including route developments, airline flight plan analysis, capacity reports over all airports and markets.
Collaborating with data analysts will help you interpret performance metrics and translate complex data into actionable business insights.
Designing and implementing research methodologies will address specific business questions and support strategic decision-making across the organisation.


About You


You have experience in market research, ideally within the airline or travel sector.
Your quantitative and qualitative analysis abilities enable you to work with large datasets and derive meaningful insights.
Proficiency in tools such as Excel, SPSS, Tableau, or equivalent analytics software supports your analytical work.
Knowledge of the global aviation market and airline industry dynamics provides context for your research.
Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities help you tackle complex challenges effectively.
Communication abilities with capacity to present complex data clearly ensure your insights are understood by stakeholders.
Experience in designing and implementing research methodologies demonstrates your research expertise.
You work effectively with key stakeholders including commercial teams, analytics functions, and product development teams.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

description_full:
For over 100 years, Deutsche Grammophon has been one of the world’s most prestigious and influential music companies: We are the market leader in classical music. Our artists include stars such as Anne-Sophie Mutter, Anna Netrebko, Daniel Barenboim, and Rolando Villazón, as well as promising newcomers like Daniil Trifonov, Alice Sara Ott, and Camille Thomas. We also represent the outstanding artists and catalogs of the Decca, Mercury Classics, and ECM labels.

Analytics, big data and cloud technologies are essential to our business. In the world of digital distribution channels and social media, we have access to more information than ever before. In the area of data analytics, we conduct in-depth analyses to better understand the listening behaviour of music fans and thus optimally market our artists and improve the listening experience. The findings of our analysis have strategic implications, such as the engagement of talented artists, the design of marketing activities and the allocation of resources.

We are seeking a highly analytical and problem solver-oriented individual to join our team for our streaming platform Stage+ . The successful candidate will be responsible for analyzing marketing campaigns and product data to provide insights and recommendations that will drive business growth and improve customer experience. The ideal candidate will be able to work cross-functionally with product and marketing teams, using data that will have a direct impact on the strategy of Stage+.

Here’s your chance to join! Effective immediately, we’re looking for a Product & Marketing Analyst (all genders) to join us at our Headquarters in Berlin. It will be a permanent full-time position.

Your Gig


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to analyze large data sets, identify trends and patterns, and provide insights and recommendations to both product and marketing teams
Develop and implement tracking concepts in our website and apps (mobile and TV)
Conduct A/B tests and experiments to optimize user experience on Stage+; as well as A/B tests for marketing campaigns across different channels
You work closely in the product development process, supporting the definition of success criteria and conduct analyses to support hypothesis
Stay closely to the marketing team, conducting analysis of marketing campaigns to evaluate their effectiveness and ROI, identify areas for improvement, and recommend optimizations to drive performance
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to track and measure the success of Stage+'s KPIs
Stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies in marketing and product analytics and provide recommendations on how to incorporate them into our processes


We Are Keen To Jam With You If You


Have a Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Marketing Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, or Business Administration; a master's degree is a plus
You gained at least 2-3 years of experience in product analytics and marketing analytics, preferably in a consumer-facing industry (ideally in a platform / app / subscription-based environment)
You are experienced with Google Tag Manager, Mixpanel, Adjust and data visualization tools
You understand basics in finance to match performance data to our business success metrics
Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and deliver high-quality results on time
Knowledge of SQL, Python and Clarisights are a plus for this role
You feel confident and comfortable reading, writing, and speaking English; German skills are a plus but the willingness is to learn the language is a must


Your Personality Matters


You have excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams
You feel most comfortable working within a dynamic environment and enjoy working towards a common goal together with interdisciplinary stakeholders
You value your excellent abilities in analytical thinking and out-of-the-box thinking
Your positivity and charisma inspire the people around you and enable you to transfer your knowledge in a highly engaging manner
You thrive working in a team that shares a growth- as well as hands-on-mindset and you would choose teamwork over heroism anytime


Our Values, Our Compass, Our Culture


Creativity – Home to Artists, Innovators and Entrepreneurs.
Connection – We are ONE team.
Passion – Music is our first love.
Responsibility – It’s up to us.
Diversity – Music is universal.
Factuition – Guided by head AND heart.


Our Benefits – Because You’re Important To Us


Feel the music! – Thanks to a concert ticket budget, streaming service vouchers, employee discounts on physical music and merchandise products as well as various internal events
Enjoy flexibility! – Thanks to our hybrid work model you benefit from high flexibility and room for collaboration and interactions in our headquarter in Berlin
Time to relax! – You are more than your job, benefit from 30 vacation days plus fixed company closure days
Always on track! - Keep moving sustainably through the capital thanks to special rates for BVG tickets (public transport) and our job bike leasing offer
Develop yourself! - We offer professional development through various workshops and courses
Self-care matters! – We promote and support mental health with our Employee Assistance Program for all challenges in life (professional and private); we offer confidential counseling and coaching through a third-party provider


#joinuniversalmusic

We look forward to receiving your application via our online application form! Please upload your documents (letter of motivation / cover letter, CV, references) as files and state your earliest starting date as well as salary expectations.

MUSIC IS UNIVERSAL!

As a large and globally operating company, Universal Music promotes diversity and opposes discrimination. We aspire to reflect the diversity represented by our artists through their music and individuality in our teams. We pledge ourselves to be an equal opportunity employer and enable individuals to express themselves – regardless of age, sex, gender, appearance, origin, faith, physical/mental ability, or sexual orientation.

If you are unsure about submitting an application because, for example, you do not fulfil all of the requirements, we would like to encourage you to try it nonetheless!

#YOUatUMG

description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

This role is based in Bangkok, Thailand, and is open to candidates worldwide. We offer a generous relocation package to ensure a smooth transition to living and working in Bangkok.

Get to Know our Team:

The Performance Marketing Team of Agoda is a world leader in online marketing. This department is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale marketing programs that improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers through measurable marketing programs and channels. The team is a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists, and data scientists in the world. The marketing leadership at Agoda have deep experience in data science, product, strategy, and other marketing fields and have built an organization that thrives on data, creative ideas, and technology. The Performance Marketing Team also fosters a great learning environment. You will be able to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a variety of backgrounds from all over the world.

In this Role, you’ll get to:


Search: Experiment with text ads, bidding, and campaign structures on Google, Bing, Baidu, Naver, and other search engines. Adapt to new product features and roll out changes from successful tests.
Display: Test, analyze, and optimize campaigns on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and others.
Modeling: Analyze the vast amounts of data generated by experiments, develop models we can use for optimization, and build dashboards for account managers.


What you’ll Need to Succeed:


Bachelor’s Degree or higher from top university in a quantitative subject (computer science, mathematics, economics, engineering, statistics or science)
Ability to communicate fluently in English
Exposure to one or more data analysis packages or databases, e.g., SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL, Tableau
Good numerical reasoning skills
Proficiency in Excel
Intellectual curiosity and analytical skills


It’s Great if you Have:


Experience in digital marketing
Academic research experience
Experience in R studio, data modeling, hypothesis testing is a plus

description_full:
About HOLY


HOLY is the soft drink revolution: With our powdered drinks HOLY Energy®, HOLY Hydration®, HOLY Iced Tea®, and HOLY Milkshake® you get amazing taste across 50+ flavors while maintaining a good conscience—for your body & the environment.
Over 2M loyal customers: Started in a shared apartment by three best friends, we have scaled to Europe's fastest growing consumer brand—and our cult-like community, the HOLY Squad, loves it: 4.6 stars on reviews.io speak for themselves.
From D2C to Global Omnichannel: In less than four years, we've profitably scaled HOLY to more than €150M D2C revenue in DACH, FR, UK and PL. Now scaling our retail business, we're on an exciting transition to becoming a true omnichannel rocketship.
We are still just getting started: Backed by the world's best consumer investors, we aim to revolutionize the soft drink industry and build a generational love brand: tastier, healthier & more sustainable. Our team of 150+ people aims high (Coca-Cola, we're coming for you!).


About The Position

As a Marketing Analyst at HOLY, you'll be the analytical backbone of our marketing and growth teams. Your mission: Turn data into decisions by delivering accurate forecasts, actionable insights, and performance analysis that fuel HOLY's commercial engine.

Responsibilities:

Sales Forecasting & Budget Tracking


Own weekly revenue forecasts and maintain our driver-based forecasting model to keep expectations aligned with reality.
Track marketing spend and budget pacing, ensuring optimal allocation across channels and campaigns.
Partner with Finance and Growth teams to optimize HOLY's commercial planning and resource allocation.


Ad-hoc Analytics & Campaign Performance Insights


Deliver rapid-response analysis for marketing teams, including campaign debriefs, performance deep dives, and channel troubleshooting.
Build and maintain dashboards that empower teams to make faster, smarter decisions
Turn complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that drive ROI and customer acquisition efficiency.


Incrementality & Marketing Channel Analytics


Design and execute incrementality tests to measure true marketing effectiveness across channels.
Provide data-driven recommendations to improve marketing ROI and customer acquisition strategy.
Analyze channel performance, identify optimization opportunities, and support strategic decision-making.


Order Volume Forecasting


Build and maintain short-term demand forecasts that factor in promotional impact and offering changes.
Model campaign volume scenarios to support inventory planning and fulfillment operations.
Help optimize resource allocation across the business by connecting marketing activity to operational needs.


Cross-Functional Operating Cadence


Prepare weekly and monthly performance meetings with clear, compelling analysis that supports management decisions.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, CRM, Finance, E-Commerce, and Operations teams.
Proactively flag risks, spot opportunities, and drive follow-through on your recommendations.


About You


Customer and business intuition: Connecting data to HOLY's product, customer behavior, and business model comes naturally, supported by 2–4 years of hands-on experience in analytics, BI, or data science (ideally in e-commerce, D2C, or consumer goods).
Analytical rigor meets speed: A strong 80/20 mindset helps you balance depth and pace, delivering fast and reliable analysis without unnecessary complexity. You have a proven ability to build forecasting models, run incrementality testing, and turn performance data into actionable insights.
Technical proficiency: Proficiency in SQL and advanced spreadsheet modeling (Google Sheets or Excel) is expected, along with experience in BI tools like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau. Bonus points for Python or R.
Clear communicator: Complex insights are translated into crisp, compelling stories for different audiences and stakeholders. Fluency in English is required.
Ownership mindset: A proactive approach to spotting issues, flagging them early, and driving recommendations through to action is essential. You do not wait to be asked twice.


Our Offering

In today's job market, A-players have many options to choose from. As part of our offer, we prioritise providing a productive working environment and opportunities for growth. In addition to competitive compensation, including company shares, you can expect:


A successful and growing company: Join a company that has been growing profitable and quickly for the last 36+ months and have raised a series B, while many other e-commerce companies are struggling.
Huge impact: Enjoy huge impact to shape HOLY’s internationalisation in a top market.
Awesome team: Join an awesome team of colleagues who are ambitious, funny, down-to-earth, and authentic. Get to know us and see for yourself!
Top product: Marketing is important, but a good product is essential. Our differentiated soft drinks have won over 2.5 million customers. Check out our reviews.io reviews or contact us for free samples.
Startup culture: Join a startup culture that values flat hierarchies, fast pace, and personal development. With us, you can make a real impact.
Perfect setup: Enjoy a perfect setup that includes all the requirements of a modern employer, such as a central office in Berlin Prenzlauerberg, flexible working hours, and much more.
Competitive comp. + equity: Join us at a pivotal stage of hypergrowth. We offer a strong salary and meaningful equity—giving you the chance to share in our current success and benefit from the massive upside as we scale.
How we support your growth: flexible hours, 28 vacation days, 30 work-from-abroad days, €1,500 learning budget, Urban Sports Club membership, public transport subsidy, and biannual 360° feedback tied to your compensation.


Find out more about HOLY and our team on our careers page.

The Process

As we are experiencing rapid growth and have ambitious plans, the optimal start date for the position is today. Nonetheless, we are prepared to patiently await the arrival of the ideal candidate, recognizing that quality ultimately yields the best results.


Getting to know you: You will have a 30 minute screening interview with Eric (Talent Acquisition Manager), where we will talk to you more about HOLY and the position and see if our expectations match.
Hiring Manager interview with Martijn: Diving deeper into some more technical topics and your experience with Martijn our Sr. Business Operations & Analytics, our Sr. Talent Development Manager.
Case study & on-site interview: As soon as you have successfully completed the first interviews, you will receive a case study. In a second meeting at our office, the team will discuss your case study with you and answer questions about our workspace, the HOLY Temple.
Founders Interview: As a strong cultural fit is important to us, you will have a final meeting with Phil , one of our HOLY Founders. This gives both you and us the opportunity for a final check.
Job offer and decision: If everything goes well, you will receive an offer and we are ready to embark you this journey together with you!


Typically his process should take around 2 weeks.

Any questions or concerns?

If you have any questions or feedback, please send an email to jobs@holy.com.

description_full:
Position Overview

Über den Bereich

In der Privatkundenbank bündelt die Deutsche Bank ihre Expertise im Privatkundengeschäft in einem Geschäftsbereich – mit den Marken Deutsche Bank und Postbank als Marktführer in Deutschland und einem globalen Netzwerk, die das Geschäft der Bank mit vermögenden Kund*innen weltweit sowie mit Privatkund*innen und kleinen bis mittelgroßen Unternehmen umfasst. In ihrem Heimatmarkt Deutschland wie auch international bietet die Privatkundenbank ihren Kund*innen hochwertige Finanzdienstleistungen aus einer Hand – von umfassenden Angeboten für Privatkund*innen über die Betreuung anspruchsvoller Unternehmer und ihrer Familien bis hin zu Lösungen für kleinere und mittelgroße Unternehmen in wichtigen Märkten der Eurozone. Die Privatkundenbank ist neben der Unternehmensbank , der Investmentbank und dem Asset Management mit ihrem Vermögensverwalter DWS eine der vier Geschäftsbereiche des Deutsche-Bank-Konzerns. Die Deutsche Bank ist Deutschlands führende Bank mit globalem Netzwerk und fest verwurzelt in Europa.

Du bist für den Bereich Marketing Daily Banking (Konto, Karte, Einlagen und Digitale Services) und Marketing Experiences (Kooperationen) für die Privatkundenbank (Deutschland) verantwortlich.

Deine Aufgaben


Du setzt den Maßstab für Performance – definierst, konsolidierst und governst ein ganzheitliches KPI‑Framework für Media, Web und CX und verankerst es wirksam in allen Marketingprozessen.
Du schaffst Transparenz, die steuert – entwickelst aussagekräftige Dashboards und Reportings, die die Performance verständlich machen und fundierte Entscheidungen ermöglichen.
Du verwandelst Daten in echte Insights – analysierst Kampagnen‑, Channel‑ und Journey‑Performance und bereitest klare Readouts für Marketing‑ und Sales‑Stakeholder auf.
Du bist der Go‑to‑Partner für Performance-Fragen – agierst als zentrale Anlaufstelle für KPI‑ und Messlogiken und arbeitest eng mit Media, CX und Data Analytics zusammen.
Du denkst Performance einen Schritt voraus – nutzt Predictive und KI‑gestützte Analytics (z. B. Forecasting, Segmentierung, Anomalieerkennung), um Trends frühzeitig zu erkennen und Optimierungen zu priorisieren.
Du berätst auf Augenhöhe – unterstützt Marketing Manager bei KPI‑Definition, Messlogik und Performance‑Interpretation und etablierst value‑basierte Steuerung (z. B. CLV) als Entscheidungsgrundlage.



Deine Fähigkeiten und Erfahrungen


Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium, idealerweise mit Schwerpunkt Marketing, Medien, Kommunikation oder Wirtschaft.
Du bringst nachweisliche Erfahrung in Marketing‑, Media‑ und Web‑Analytics mit und steuerst sowie interpretierst Performance‑KPIs sicher im Marketingkontext.
Du gehst routiniert mit Datenanalyse, Segmentierung und Forecasting um – inklusive Search‑ und Paid‑Logiken sowie modellbasierter Prognosen.
Du verfügst über starke Expertise in Data Visualization & BI und entwickelst aussagekräftige, stakeholdergerechte Dashboards (z. B. Power BI oder Tableau).
Du setzt Predictive und KI‑gestützte Analytics praxisnah ein, um Insights zu skalieren – etwa durch automatisierte Auswertungen, Pattern Detection oder Prognosen.
Du verstehst CLV‑Modelle und Werttreiberlogiken und nutzt sie, um Marketingmaßnahmen daten‑ und value‑basiert zu priorisieren.
Du überzeugst durch analytische Stärke, Präzision und Ergebnisorientierung, kommunizierst klar, übersetzt Daten in handlungsrelevante Empfehlungen, arbeitest gern im Team – und verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse.



Was wir Dir bieten

Wir bieten eine breite Palette von Leistungen, die all Ihre beruflichen und persönlichen Bedürfnisse abdecken.


Emotional ausgeglichen Eine positive Haltung hilft uns, die Herausforderungen des Alltags zu meistern – beruflich wie privat. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beratung in schwierigen Lebenssituationen und Angeboten zur Förderung mentaler Gesundheit.
Körperlich fit Mit Angeboten zur Aufrechterhaltung Ihrer persönlichen Gesundheit und einem förderlichen beruflichen Umfeld hilft Ihnen die Bank, körperlich fit zu bleiben. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie umfangreichen Check-up Untersuchungen, Impfangeboten und Beratung zur gesunden Lebensführung.
Sozial vernetzt Der Austausch mit anderen eröffnet uns neue Perspektiven, bringt uns beruflich wie persönlich voran und stärkt unser Selbstvertrauen und Wohlbefinden. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Unterstützung durch den pme Familienservice, das FitnessCenter Job, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle (bspw. Teilzeit, Jobtandem, hybrides Arbeiten) sowie einer umfangreichen Kultur der Vielfalt, Chancengleichheit und Teilhabe.
Finanziell abgesichert Die Bank sichert Sie nicht nur während Ihrer aktiven Karriere, sondern auch für die Zukunft finanziell ab und unterstützt Ihre Flexibilität sowie Mobilität – egal ob privat oder beruflich. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beitragsplänen für Altersvorsorge, Bankdienstleistungen für Mitarbeiter*innen, Firmenfahrrad oder dem Deutschlandticket.



Da die Benefits je nach Standort geringfügig variieren, gehe bitte bei konkreten Fragen auf Deinen Recruiter / Deine Recruiterin zu.

Wir streben eine Unternehmenskultur an, in der wir gemeinsam jeden Tag das Beste geben. Dazu gehören verantwortungsvolles Handeln, wirtschaftliches Denken, Initiative ergreifen und zielgerichtete Zusammenarbeit.

Gemeinsam teilen und feiern wir die Erfolge unserer Mitarbeiter*innen. Gemeinsam sind wir die Deutsche Bank Gruppe.

Wir begrüßen Bewerbungen von allen Menschen und fördern ein positives, faires und integratives Arbeitsumfeld.

description_full:
Your Mission




We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Manager who wants to do more than just run campaigns — someone who’s excited to directly shape how companies source and manufacture parts through MakerVerse. In this role, you’ll own our digital acquisition engine end-to-end, turning high-intent B2B demand into real business impact. From the first search to the final order, you’ll optimize every step of the funnel across Search, PMax, and Display — driving qualified leads and revenue for our Account Executives.




Your Impact




Drive Growth: Execute and optimize paid media campaigns to accelerate customer acquisition and order entry on makerverse.com.
Funnel Optimization: Collaborate with the Platform team to ensure web events are correctly tracked in Mixpanel and HubSpot, enabling a seamless data flow.
Sales Enablement: Work closely with the Sales team to ensure campaigns target the right industries (e.g. Aerospace, MedTech) and attract high-value decision-makers.
Conversion Optimization: Build and optimize landing pages in Unbounce and WordPress to maximize conversion rates from paid traffic.
Paid Search Focus: Continuously improve campaign structure, targeting, and performance across Search, PMax, and Display to capture high-intent demand.
Collaboration: Maintain a strong feedback loop across teams, sharing insights to continuously improve performance.







Your DNA

description_full:
Based In Frankfurt

2 days working from home, three days in the office




The Change Partners are working with a global leader in the home wellness and sleep technology sector. We are looking for an ambitious, "pricing-first" Global Performance & Value Manager to join their Category Performance, Pricing & Market Intelligence team in Lisbon.




You will be the bridge between complex data and actionable strategy. Your mission is to provide the data, frameworks, and strategic insights that make better portfolio decisions possible across the full product lifecycle.




Your Responsibilities:




Category Performance Intelligence: Design and own global performance dashboards in partnership with BI teams, tracking revenue, margin, and NPS correlation by SKU and channel.




Pricing Architecture: Design and maintain global price ladders, pricing tiers, and trade-up logic to ensure portfolio coherence across all market clusters.
Value Capture: Maintain a pricing elasticity model and conduct regular price-value assessments to ensure products are priced relative to consumer-perceived value.




Promotional Governance: Define promo guardrails and build tracking models to measure actual ROI vs. planned, protecting the brand from margin erosion.
Competitive Intelligence: Own a structured view of the competitive landscape, tracking rival pricing, launches, and promotional activity across key markets.




Market Intelligence: Develop repeatable processes for market sizing and category growth analysis to identify white-space opportunities and strategic risks.




Business Case Support: Serve as the analytical partner to Global and Regional Category Managers, building validated business cases for new product investments and expansion.




Analytics Empowerment: Develop self-service tools and templates that help the broader category team make faster, data-backed decisions.




What We’re Looking For:




Experience: 5–8 years in pricing, business analytics, revenue management, or category performance within a consumer goods, DTC, or e-commerce context.




Pricing Strategy: A proven track record of designing pricing frameworks and understanding the interplay between pricing, perceived value, and margin.




Data Literacy: Highly analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into clear, decision-ready business recommendations.




Statistical Foundation: Solid understanding of statistics (hypothesis testing, regressions) and clustering methods like k-means.




Data Visualisation: Proven experience building performance dashboards in BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.




Technical Proficiency: Working knowledge of Python for data analysis (pandas, data cleaning, and exploratory analysis).




Please note this role is based in Frankfurt, and you’ll need the full right to work in Germany

Relocation package support is not available

Manage interactions with Ground Ops colleagues and Data & Analytics teams to develop performance dashboards and ensure Ground Ops data requirements are met

Proven operational experience and understanding of Ground Operations
Leadership skills, with the ability to motivate, engage and develop a team to deliver results
Results-driven individual, with strong data and analytical skills
Strong communications skills, being able to take multiple data sets and convey these clearly and in multiple formats to various audiences
Customer focused individual, driven to achieve the best possible standards for our customers
Good interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trust, rapport, and productive relationships
Ability to influence peers and senior leaders, achieving buy-in to new initiatives
Well-organised, with strong process management skills and an ability to establish and work to priorities
Self-motivated and ability to work independently as well as part of a team
Excellent IT skills and experience using analytics software (Tableau), Excel and PowerPoint
Role requires strong business English, both spoken and written, and will involve occasional international travel


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

SECURITY STATEMENT

At TUI, safety is our top priority. We conduct thorough background checks on all employees who join our Airline. Rest assured; we’ll guide you on the necessary information you need to provide at each stage.

Please note that if you are applying for a role in the UK, you must ensure you can provide a full 5 years referencing, plus a criminal record check for all countries you’ve resided in for more than 6 months in the past 5 years (some costs may apply)

description_full:
HOYER GmbH

Wir gehören zu den weltweit führenden Anbietern von Logistiklösungen für die Chemie-, Mineralöl-, Gas- und Lebensmittelindustrie, aufbauend auf unseren Kernkompetenzen. Als Familienunternehmen stehen wir hinter jedem einzelnen unserer weltweit 3.800 Mitarbeiter. Ihr Können, Engagement und Know-how sind die Basis dafür, dass wir immer einen Schritt weiter gehen und unseren Kunden stets die beste Lösung liefern. Für den Bereich Business Intelligence suchen wir einen Business Intelligence Manager (m/w/d) am Standort Hamburg, der datengetriebene Entscheidungen auf das nächste Level hebt.

Deine Aufgaben


Gemeinsam mit den Fachbereichen entwickelst du datengetriebene Analyse- und Reporting-Lösungen, die unsere Transportlogistik nachhaltig optimieren

Zudem analysierst du globale Transportdaten und entwickelst Prognose- sowie Optimierungsmodelle auf Basis von Machine-Learning-Methoden
Du arbeitest an Advanced-Analytics- und AI-Use-Cases zur Optimierung des Transportnetzwerks sowie zur Kapazitäts- und Auslastungssteuerung und überführst diese gemeinsam mit den Fachbereichen in datenbasierte Entscheidungsmodelle
Ein weiterer Schwerpunkt ist deine aktive Mitarbeit an der Architektur, Modellierung und Weiterentwicklung des neuen Data Warehouses, einschließlich der ETL/ELT‑Prozesse
In enger Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineering und den Fachbereichen bringst du zudem analytische Best Practices ins Unternehmen ein.


Dein Können


Abgeschlossenes Studium in Data Science, Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Statistik oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang
Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich Business Intelligence, Data Analytics oder Data Science – idealerweise im Logistik‑ oder Industrieumfeld
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL, Datenmodellierung sowie in ETL‑/ELT‑Prozessen
Erfahrung mit BI‑Tools (z. B. Qlik, Power BI oder vergleichbar) sowie ein gutes Verständnis moderner Analytics‑Architekturen
Kenntnisse in statistischen Methoden und idealerweise erste praktische Erfahrung mit Machine Learning oder AI‑basierten Modellen
Ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Daten in klare Business‑Insights zu übersetzen
Sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse für die Zusammenarbeit in einem internationalen Umfeld

Anspruchsvolle und eigenverantwortliche Aufgabenbereiche
Personalentwicklung, die Fort- und Weiterbildungen fördert

We provide B2B support services across multiple brands, partners, and markets. We operate in a scale-up environment and value people who are commercially aware, fast-moving, collaborative, and motivated by impact.




Role Overview:

We are looking for a Senior Performance Marketing Analyst to take ownership of channel performance analytics across multiple brands.

You will
description_full:
Who We Are:

Our company has a vision to change people’s mood for the better. Our mission is to be a fast-moving, highly efficient tech services business that engages people, partners, and players and delivers great experiences.

We provide B2B support services across multiple brands, partners, and markets. We operate in a scale-up environment and value people who are commercially aware, fast-moving, collaborative, and motivated by impact.




Role Overview:

We are looking for a Senior Performance Marketing Analyst to take ownership of channel performance analytics across multiple brands.

This role sits at the intersection of marketing, data, and commercial decision-making - with a focus on understanding where growth comes from, how it performs, and how to scale it profitably.

You will be responsible for building a clear view of the acquisition funnel, linking marketing spend to customer behaviour and value, and enabling the business to make data-driven budget decisions.




Key Responsibilities:

1. Channel Performance & Acquisition Analytics

Build and maintain a full view of the top-of-funnel acquisition funnel
Analyse performance across Meta, affiliates, SEO, and other channels
Identify high-performing vs underperforming campaigns, sources, and geos
Partner with marketing teams to optimise budget allocation and campaign strategy




2. Marketing Attribution & Measurement

Ensure accurate tracking of:
Campaigns (UTMs, gclid/fbclid)
Traffic sources
Conversion events
Translate marketing activity into CPA, ROAS, and payback insights
Own and develop attribution models (starting with last-touch, evolving over time)




3. Data Integration & Modelling

Work with BigQuery and internal data sources to:
Combine marketing data with customer behaviour and revenue
Build datasets that connect spend → user → value
Collaborate with BI/engineering to ensure clean, scalable data pipelines
Define and maintain consistent KPI definitions across the business




4. Stakeholder Collaboration

Work closely with:
Performance Marketing
CRM
Product / CRO
BI & Data Engineering
Translate business questions into analytical frameworks and outputs
Challenge stakeholders on what “good” looks like and align on success metrics




5. Reporting & Insights

Build and maintain dashboards (Tableau / similar) for:
CPA, ROAS, conversion rates
Funnel performance
Channel comparisons
Deliver clear, actionable insights, not just reporting
Support weekly/monthly performance reviews with data-driven narratives




What We’re Looking For:

Experience

5+ years in performance marketing analytics, growth analytics, or marketing data
Strong experience with paid media data and acquisition funnel analysis
Experience working in multi-channel environments

Technical Skills

Strong SQL skills (BigQuery or similar)
Experience working with large-scale marketing datasets
Good understanding of:
Attribution modelling
GA4, GTM, UTMs
CPA, ROAS, LTV
Marketing data pipelines (Fivetran or similar is a plus)

Mindset

Commercially driven - understands what drives revenue, not just metrics
Able to work with imperfect data and still produce insight
Strong ownership - able to define problems and solutions independently
Clear communicator - able to explain complex data to non-technical stakeholders




What Success Looks Like (3-6 months):

A clear, trusted view of channel performance across key brands
Reliable CPA and ROAS reporting by campaign/source
A working acquisition funnel from click to revenue
Stakeholders using data to make budget and campaign decisions confidently




Why Join Us:

This is an opportunity to play a key role in shaping how marketing performance is measured across multiple brands.

You will have visibility across spend, performance, and customer value, and your work will directly support better investment decisions, stronger campaign performance, and smarter growth.

If you want a role where you can combine analytics, commercial thinking, and real business impact, this is a strong opportunity to do it.

description_full:
Evotec is seeking a dynamic

Market Research Lead (all genders)

Full time and permanent

Role Overview

The Market Research Lead will lead Evotec’s market research strategy within the Competitive Intelligence function, acting as the primary driver of actionable insights on market dynamics, customer needs, and competitive positioning. This role is responsible for designing and implementing frameworks that monitor industry trends, market opportunities, and competitor strategies to inform strategic decisions across corporate and business segments. Reporting directly to the SVP, Head of Competitive Intelligence, the Director will collaborate closely with the CI Leadership Team to ensure market intelligence is fully integrated with science & commercial insights and business analytics, strengthening Evotec’s competitive advantage and supporting sustainable growth.

Responsibilities


Market Research Strategy & Alignment
Define and execute Evotec’s market intelligence strategy in alignment with corporate priorities and business objectives.
Translate market trends and competitive signals into actionable recommendations for portfolio strategy, commercial positioning, and growth initiatives.
Support annual and long-range planning with market forecasts, opportunity assessments, and scenario analyses.
Competitive & Market Analysis
Lead comprehensive analysis of industry trends, therapeutic areas, and competitor strategies across global markets.
Monitor pricing benchmarks, business models, and partnership structures to inform Evotec’s commercial approach.
Deliver forward-looking insights on emerging technologies, regulatory shifts, and market disruptions impacting Evotec’s positioning.
Intelligence Integration & Collaboration
Work closely with the CI Leadership Team to integrate market intelligence with scientific insights and business analytics.
Partner with BD, Commercial Strategy, R&D and Operations teams to embed market insights into decision-making processes.
Support strategic projects such as portfolio prioritization, go-to-market strategies, and partnership evaluations.
Tools, Methodologies & Best Practices
Champion best practices in market intelligence methodologies, tools, and data sources.
Ensure governance and consistency in market data collection, validation, and reporting.
Drive adoption of advanced CI tools and platforms to enhance insight generation.
Strategic Communication & Leadership Support
Prepare and deliver high-impact reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior stakeholders.
Communicate complex market insights in a clear, actionable manner to diverse audiences.
Represent Market Research in internal forums as a thought leader.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Life Sciences, Economics, or related discipline; MBA preferred.
Substantial experience in market intelligence, competitive intelligence, or strategic planning within biotech, pharma, or CRO/CDMO sectors.
Proven track record of delivering market insights that influence corporate strategy and commercial decisions.
Strong understanding of drug discovery and development value chain, market dynamics, and competitive landscape.
Proficiency in CI and market intelligence tools (e.g., Evaluate, GlobalData, Pitchbook), and BI platforms (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Exceptional analytical, strategic thinking, and communication skills.
Ability to influence senior stakeholders and manage cross-functional projects in a global matrix environment.
Market Analysis Expertise: Strong capability in industry trend monitoring, competitor benchmarking, and scenario planning.
Strategic Thinking: Ability to translate market signals into actionable recommendations.
Tool Proficiency: CI and market intelligence platforms (Evaluate, GlobalData, Pitchbook).
Collaboration: Work cross-functionally with BD, R&D, Operations and Commercial Strategy.
Presentation Skills: Deliver high-impact reports and dashboards for senior stakeholders.
Adaptability: Navigate dynamic market conditions and regulatory changes.


Our Offer


A position within a vigorous and exciting professional environment promoted by an open culture and a spirit of community
A diverse, international workforce with a dynamic working environment that fosters creativity, innovations and teamwork
30 days of annual holiday, monthly allowance for public transportation, and in-house canteen
Capital forming benefits, flexible working hours, holiday pay, and annual bonus depending on performance


To apply, please click on the “Apply” button and provide your application documents (CV and cover letter, including earliest possible start date and salary requirements). We are looking forward to getting to know you and to your application.

FR : Dans le cadre de sa politique Diversité, Evotec étudie, à compétences égales, toutes les candidatures dont celles des personnes en situation de handicap.

ENG : In the frame of our Diversity policy, Evotec considers, with equal competences, all applications including people with disabilities.

description_full:
Hi. Wir sind Vivaldi - eine weltweit führende Strategieberatung für Marketing, Wachstum und Innovation. Dabei sind wir mehr als eine traditionelle Strategieberatung und gestalten die nächste Generation von Marken und Unternehmen. Wir glauben daran, dass Beratung über reine Strategiearbeit hinausdenken und mit umsetzbaren Ergebnissen einen echten Impact erzielen muss. Dafür arbeiten wir mit Empathie und Leidenschaft, damit wir Unternehmen unterstützen können, außerhalb ihrer eigenen Branchengrenzen zu denken und neue, relevante Geschäftsmodelle am Markt zu etablieren.

Die Suche nach neuen Wachstumspfaden endet nicht in der Zusammenarbeit mit Unternehmen, sondern ist Teil unserer DNA als globales Team. Wir bieten allen Mitarbeiter*innen Raum und Möglichkeiten, sich zu entfalten und jeden Tag ein Stück über sich hinauszuwachsen.

Neben unserem Hauptsitz in New York sind wir an vielen europäischen Standorten vertreten. Derzeit suchen wir Toptalente zur Verstärkung unseres Teams in München.

Das erwartet dich


Unterstützung des gesamten Projektablaufes - von der Analyse, über die Strategieentwicklung bis hin zur Umsetzung
Eigenständige Bearbeitung klar definierter Aufgabenpakete innerhalb von Projekten
Aufbereitung von Markt-, Marken- und Wettbewerbsanalysen
Strukturierung und Verdichtung von Analyseergebnissen sowie Ableitung erster Implikationen und Handlungsempfehlungen
Mitwirkung bei der Entwicklung von Markenstrategien, Konzepten und innovativen Geschäftsmodellen
Unterstützung bei der Vorbereitung, Organisation und Nachbereitung von Terminen und Workshops mit Klient*innen
Erstellung fundierter Präsentationen, Reports und Entscheidungsgrundlagen
Mitarbeit bei internen Projekten, Thought Leadership-Themen sowie Unterstützung bei New Business-Aktivitäten


Das bringst du mit


Einen überdurchschnittlich guten Diplom- oder Masterabschluss im Bereich Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder Marketing
Erste praktische Erfahrungen / Praktika im Bereich Marketing, Brand Management oder Consulting
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten sowie ein strukturiertes und sorgfältiges Vorgehen
Erste Erfahrung in der Aufbereitung und Interpretation von Daten und Analysen
Gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und Freude an der Zusammenarbeit im Team
Selbstständige Arbeitsweise, Pioniergeist und die Fähigkeit, schnell und zuverlässig Verantwortung zu übernehmen
Begeisterung für Marken, Marketing und Innovationsthemen und Interesse an persönlicher Weiterentwicklung im Beratungs-Umfeld
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse sowie sichere MS-Office-Kenntnisse


Warum Vivaldi

Bei uns gestaltest du dir deine Karriere. Wir bieten dir ein unternehmerisches Arbeits- und Lernumfeld, in dem du dich frei entfalten kannst. Als Teil unserer internationalen Beratungsteams arbeitest du projektbasiert mit führenden Unternehmen zusammen - von globalen Corporates bis hin zu mittelständischen Firmen im B2B- sowie B2C-Bereich. Du treibst Themen eigenverantwortlich voran und entwickelst Lösungsansätze auf Augenhöhe mit unseren Expert*innen und Partner*innen von Tag 1 an.

Bist du bereit für deine nächste Wachstumschance? Dann werde Teil unseres Teams. Wir freuen uns auf dich!

Folge uns auf Instagram und bekomme einen ersten Einblick in die Arbeit bei Vivaldi.
